items.author: Aditya Agarwal
items.bio: South Park Commons GP, ex-Dropbox CTO
items.content_zh: Agarwal 感叹每天都能发现有人让 LLM 做出奇特有趣的事情，不断提醒自己：我们仍处于这项神奇技术的超级早期阶段。
items.content_en: Agarwal marvels that every day someone manages to get LLMs to do something weird and interesting. A reminder that we're still in the super early innings of figuring out what we can build with this technology.

items.author: Alex Albert
items.content_zh: Alex Albert 表示 Claude Code 桌面端新版本带来了大量新功能和性能改进。他发现在 Cowork 和 Code 之间切换，已经不太需要打开其他应用甚至终端来完成大部分工作了。
items.content_en: Alex Albert highlights the new Claude Code desktop release with tons of new features and performance improvements. Between Cowork and Code, he's found he barely needs to open other apps — or even his terminal — to do most of his work now.

items.author: Amjad Masad
items.content_zh: Masad 感叹以前需要单独构建的一次性转换工具，在 Replit 上现在只是一个 skill，体现了 AI 编程平台降低开发门槛的趋势。
items.content_en: Masad notes that one-off conversion apps he used to build individually are now just a skill on Replit — reflecting how AI coding platforms are collapsing development effort.

items.author: Amjad Masad
items.content_zh: Replit 与 Razorpay 合作，印度开发者现在可以直接在 Replit 上构建应用并使用 Razorpay 支付，进一步拓展印度市场。
items.content_en: Replit partners with Razorpay, enabling Indian builders to build and accept payments directly on Replit — expanding further into the Indian market.

items.author: Cat Wu
items.content_zh: Cat Wu 介绍了 Cowork 的新特性，包括多窗口工作流、在线协作、可重新排列的工作面板等功能，大大提升了开发者的工作效率。
items.content_en: Cat Wu introduces Cowork's new features including multi-window workflows, online collaboration, and rearrangeable work panels — major productivity enhancements for builders.

items.author: Peter Yang
items.bio: Product at Roblox, AI newsletter author
items.content_zh: Peter Yang 发出疑问：Claude 几乎每隔一天就出现一次服务中断，究竟是发布节奏过快、算力扩容跟不上，还是另有原因？这是对前沿 AI 大规模部署可靠性的尖锐追问。
items.content_en: Peter Yang wonders whether Claude's seemingly daily outages are caused by Anthropic's shipping pace, compute scaling, or something deeper — a pointed question about reliability tradeoffs at the frontier of AI deployment.

items.author: Peter Yang
items.bio: Product at Roblox, AI newsletter author
items.content_zh: Peter Yang 从为期两周的中国之行总结出 15 条观察，他认为每个产品构建者都应该至少来中国一次——理解中国 AI 的工作文化、电动车与 2 美元外卖生态，以及当地人真实的生活与工作方式。
items.content_en: Peter Yang shares 15 observations from a two-week trip to China, arguing every product builder should visit at least once to understand Chinese AI work culture, the EV and $2 delivery ecosystem, and how people actually live and work there.

items.author: Amjad Masad
items.content_zh: Amjad Masad 提出一个设想：当漏洞挖掘被前沿模型完全自动化后，GitHub 应该像展示 star 数一样，公开显示每个开源项目在安全加固上花费了多少算力。他认为这是开源软件在规模化时代能被信任的唯一途径。
items.content_en: Amjad Masad proposes that once finding security flaws is fully automated by frontier models, GitHub should expose a metric (like stars) showing how much compute has been spent securing each open-source package — in his view, the only way OSS can be trusted at scale.

items.author: Guillermo Rauch
items.content_zh: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 认为，每个团队都能打造自己的「设计工厂」——他以用 Claude Code、Three.js、Next.js 和 Vercel 为 basement.studio 量身定制的 Shader Lab 为例。重新组装强大构件来生成软件，已经比寻找并采购合适的 SaaS 更简单。
items.content_en: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch argues every team can now build its own 'design factory' — citing Shader Lab, built with Claude Code, Three.js, Next.js, and Vercel to basement.studio's exact vision. Generating software by reassembling powerful building blocks is now easier than procuring the right SaaS.

items.author: Peter Steinberger
items.content_zh: Peter Steinberger 警告：GPT 5.4-Cyber 对闭源代码的逆向能力已经到了令人担忧的程度。OpenClaw 的应对策略是快速迭代、代码加固并公开安全通告，他建议对那些回避安全工作、不公布漏洞通告的开源 harness 项目保持警惕。
items.content_en: Peter Steinberger warns that GPT 5.4-Cyber's closed-source reverse-engineering ability is bad news for projects that don't harden aggressively. OpenClaw's response has been rapid iteration, code hardening, and published advisories — he urges caution toward open-source harnesses that skip that work.

items.author: Aditya Agarwal
items.bio: South Park Commons GP, ex-Dropbox CTO
items.content_zh: Aditya Agarwal 勾勒了 LLM 怀疑论者的退守轨迹：「只是自动纠错」→「只是下一个 token 预测」→「它们会幻觉」→「只对训练数据有用」→「只擅长写代码」→「只擅长可验证的任务」。他的结论很直接：承认吧，LLM 就是很厉害。
items.content_en: Aditya Agarwal charts the arc of an LLM skeptic: 'just auto-correct' → 'just next-token predictors' → 'they hallucinate' → 'only useful for trained data' → 'only good for coding' → 'only good for verifiable things.' His conclusion: just admit it, LLMs are awesome.

items.author: AI & I by Every
items.bio: Dan Shipper's podcast on AI and work
items.content_zh: Logical Intelligence 创始人 Eve 与 Dan Shipper 深入讨论能量基模型（EBM）——一种非自回归、无 token 的架构，直接把数据映射到能量景观，并用潜变量存储数据中的结构性规律。核心卖点是：相对于 LLM 一次只能预测下一个 token 的「隧道视野」，EBM 具备「鸟瞰视角」，因此更便宜、可验证，特别适合空间推理、芯片设计等对正确性要求极高的任务，以及 LLM 容易幻觉的稀疏数据场景。
items.content_en: Logical Intelligence founder Eve joins Dan Shipper to unpack Energy-Based Models (EBMs) — a non-autoregressive, token-free architecture that maps data directly onto an energy landscape and stores structural rules in latent variables. The pitch: EBMs give a 'bird's-eye view' instead of LLMs' tunnel-vision token guessing, making them cheaper, verifiable, and far better suited to spatial reasoning, mission-critical correctness tasks like chip design, and sparse-data problems where LLMs hallucinate.
